name: whisper-tiny.en
license: MIT (whisper.cpp), MIT (OpenAI Whisper weights converted to ggml format)
training_data_summary: OpenAI's original Whisper tiny.en model. Trained by OpenAI on 680,000 hours of multilingual and multitask supervised data collected from the web; the tiny.en variant is the smallest English-only checkpoint (~39 MB ggml format). No human face data, no biometric data. See https://github.com/openai/whisper for the upstream training description and https://arxiv.org/abs/2212.04356 for the paper.
conversion_pipeline: openai/whisper PyTorch checkpoint -> ggerganov/whisper.cpp `models/convert-pt-to-ggml.py` -> ggml-tiny.en.bin (Float16). Re-quantized to q5_0 in the same upstream release pipeline.
architecture: Encoder-decoder Transformer. 4 encoder layers, 4 decoder layers, 384-d hidden, 6 attention heads. ~39M parameters.
input_schema: Float32 mono PCM at 16 kHz. Internally chunked to 30-second windows and converted to a log-Mel spectrogram by whisper.cpp.
output_schema: Tokenized text per audio segment with start/end timestamps. The MirrorMesh `TranscriptFrame` wraps one such segment.
build_status: v0.3.0 ships a mocked Whisper backend (see docs/voice-pipeline.md). The model file is NOT committed to the repo. To use the real backend (once linked in a future drop): download from the source URL above to ~/Library/Application Support/MirrorMesh/whisper-tiny.en.bin and verify the sha256.
privacy_note: All transcription is local. No audio leaves the machine. Conforms to projectRules.md R3 (local-only on the inference hot path) and R4 (no cloud LLM/ML APIs as inference backends).
